All of a sudden, a clean exchange became real tense. I could see AaronГўВҖВҷs eyes moving from man to man, doing the math. I wanted to hurt Aaron bad. Not for what he did to meГўВҖВ”I could forgive thatГўВҖВ”but for what he did to Christine. But I can do math too and the numbers didnГўВҖВҷt add up. Aaron and I just stared at each other, hatred in our eyes, but the deal went down.  Big Boss later asked me what happened but I told him I just had a bad vibe. I stuck to the story. No sense worrying the man. Aaron must have lied to his big boss too because we had more deals and I always carried. You donГўВҖВҷt need to tell me twice.  When Aaron finally came looking for me, he shot the big boss and I killed Aaron. Or at least I tried to. Lucky shot got him in the leg. The Feds offered a deal: it was self defense if I testified, Christine and I would get the Witness Protection Program, provided I kept myself clean. If I didnГўВҖВҷt testify, IГўВҖВҷd go down.  I donГўВҖВҷt grass, but I had Christine to think about. It was a hard decision. I tried to keep my account managers out of it, but no deal. And I had to point out the big bosses. Mine survived being shot and AaronГўВҖВҷs boss went down, too. One stupid fool and everybodyГўВҖВҷs looking at me. Well, everybody except Aaron. The slippery son of a bitch was offered the deal, too. He took down his side, I took down mine.  So Christine and I live in hiding. New ID cards. New social security numbers. IГўВҖВҷm a district manager of a snack vending companyГўВҖВ”yeah, I get the ironyГўВҖВ”and Christine, bless her, didnГўВҖВҷt leave my side. She was just happy I was out.  And then we had Sarah.   Now.  YouГўВҖВҷd be surprised how many people die in the Witness Protection Program. The FBI drilled this into us repeatedly. All it takes is one moment of homesickness, one quick phone call, and if the people who want you want you bad enough, youГўВҖВҷre dead. One fool went back to his old home and turned the doorknob and it blew up.  Christine and I knew the score. Neither of us had anything to go back to. Christine became Angela and I became James. We were moved to a new city in another state. The FBI got me a job, a small house, and a phone number for emergency use only. No friends. No social circle. No way back. Pretty fuckin' shitty deal, but it beats dying.  But we had each other. ThatГўВҖВҷs enough.   Then.  Gisele died unexpectedly. She was in great health and whenever Christine wasnГўВҖВҷt with me or at school, she was with her mom. So when one day Gisele didnГўВҖВҷt answer her door, Christine panicked. She just knew something was wrong. Shortly after, we were at GiseleГўВҖВҷs funeral. Brain hemorrhage. The doctor promised us it was painless and looking at her in the casket, she looked peaceful, but then, I guess thatГўВҖВҷs the morticianГўВҖВҷs job.   Now.  IГўВҖВҷm looking at the sheet of paper and know I have to go to the FBI now, but then thereГўВҖВҷs Sarah. What do I do about her? SheГўВҖВҷs not old enough to know whatГўВҖВҷs going on and thereГўВҖВҷs no way sheГўВҖВҷll understand if we call her a different name. She wonГўВҖВҷt know why she has to leave her friends at school and AngelaГўВҖВҷs going to be terrified. So I do what I have to do. I buy a gun.  Normally, ex-cons donГўВҖВҷt get to buy guns but James Hillman is not an ex-con. I buy a shotgun to avoid the waiting period and I go home and head down to the basement. I turn on the light bulb and ignore the damp, musty smell. I open my toolbox and a few strokes of a hacksaw removes most of the barrel. A bit of work with some files and cutting down the stock and I have a nasty short-range weapon. A nasty short-range weapon thatГўВҖВҷs pretty easy to hide. Pretty illegal, too, even if James Hillman isnГўВҖВҷt an ex-con. IГўВҖВҷm going to have some trouble explaining this when I get caught.  I will get caught. I know that, but I donГўВҖВҷt care. But Christine and Sarah will be safe. I really have no choice.  I think about this a lot and I figure that if AaronГўВҖВҷs after me, heГўВҖВҷs a lone nut. Otherwise, Gisele would have said something different, right? But no, GiseleГўВҖВҷs dead. Who the hell sent that damned envelope?  AaronГўВҖВҷs Lee Harvey Oswald and IГўВҖВҷm John F-in' Kennedy. Except JFK wasnГўВҖВҷt married to OswaldГўВҖВҷs ex. JFK didnГўВҖВҷt know Oswald was coming to seek revenge for JFK being with Jackie. And I will kill Aaron and the cops will take me away and Christine and Sarah will be safe. ThatГўВҖВҷs all I care about.   Now.  I stop staying late in the office and bring my paperwork home with me. ChristineГўВҖВ”or is she Angela?ГўВҖВ”asks whatГўВҖВҷs going on and I tell her I just want to spend more time with her, even if I have to work. I thought I caught her crying once. She knows something is wrong just as she always does.  Sarah likes to crawl into my lap. I kiss her forehead and keep working, my mind trying not to think of the sawed-off shotgun in my briefcase. On a hot Saturday, IГўВҖВҷm not surprised when the third envelope falls through the slot. They always arrive when Angela/Christine and Sarah/Sarah are out.  I open it and read two words, ГўВҖВңTomorrow Night.ГўВҖВқ On the back of the envelope was a single name, ГўВҖВңLizbeth.ГўВҖВқ  That night, curled up next to Christine/Angela, I ask her a simple question.  ГўВҖВңHer name was LizbethГўВҖВқ, Christine murmurs.  But by now, I already knew that.   Now.  Sunday. SarahГўВҖВҷs best friend was always asking her to sleep over and, for the first time, I said it was a good idea. I am over-protective of Sarah. Christine thought Sarah staying with a friend was wonderful and had already started to plan a romantic evening but an emergency phone call from work took me away.  And an emergency phone call from Frank took Angela away, too. He says heГўВҖВҷs broken up with his girlfriend and is upset, but since IГўВҖВҷm not there, will Christine come? I told him to keep her away the entire night, whatever it took. IГўВҖВҷll be happy if sheГўВҖВҷs mad. Dead people donГўВҖВҷt get mad. As she drives off, I huddle down further in the bushes, the shotgun in one hand and a cell phone in my pocket.  Aaron shows up just before midnight. The lights are off and as he limps to the front of the house, I watch him, quiet as the names we never repeat any more. He walks up to the front door and feels under the mat for a key. ThatГўВҖВҷs good because I left one for him. If he breaks in, maybe I can get away with shooting him in the back. As he carefully turns the key in the knob, I raise the shotgun.  And then I put it down again. Good people might do bad things, but I couldnГўВҖВҷt do this. Aaron tiptoes inside and I lift the cell phone and call the special number the FBI gave me for situations just like this. And thatГўВҖВҷs when Christine pulls back into the driveway. And thatГўВҖВҷs when Aaron steps outside. And thatГўВҖВҷs when Aaron pulls a gun and points it at Angela.  Or is she Christine? IГўВҖВҷm confused. GiseleГўВҖВҷs in my mind telling me IГўВҖВҷm a good man who does bad things. IГўВҖВҷm not. IГўВҖВҷm a bad man trying to do good things.  I shoot Aaron in the back, rack, shoot him again, rack, shoot him again and keep going until the shotgun is empty. Christine is screaming.   Now.  You might think IГўВҖВҷd get in trouble with that shotgun, but when youГўВҖВҷre in the Witness Protection Program, some problems go away. ГўВҖВңBrave Homeowner Kills Intruder Threatening WifeГўВҖВқ read the headline. Any publicity usually means you need to leave. Publicity and knowing someone has found you guarantees it. I donГўВҖВҷt know what weГўВҖВҷll tell Sarah, but the FBI tells me they already have a plan for that.  When we move into our new house, thereГўВҖВҷs an envelope lying on the floor in the foyer. ThereГўВҖВҷs no name on it, but it has a single piece of paper with two words, ГўВҖВңThank you.ГўВҖВқ
title: Short Story: The Names We Never Say

body:
Philosophical Mathematics My Hilarious Job Interview In The Beginning The Great Philosophies. Logicism Formalism Intuitionism More Philophical Approaches. Set-theoretic Platonism (ZFC) Structuralism Finitism and Constructivism Mathematical Naturalism Why It Matters Do Mathematicians Care? Closing Thought Philosophical Mathematics IГўВҖВҷm kind of a math nerd in the sense that I love math , [1] but not in the sense that IГўВҖВҷm brilliant at it. I took calculus in my junior year of high school, a full year before my classmates, [2] . However, like so many of my contemporaries, I have forgotten much of my math. This led to one of my most hilarious job interviews, ever. My Hilarious Job Interview In 2007, I was living in London and job hunting, and someone from a local company known ominously as ГўВҖВңthe place Perl programmers go to dieГўВҖВқ was interviewing me. After a short interview, I was giving a programming test. ГўВҖВңWrite a function that, given a number, finds the prime factors.ГўВҖВқ I had to do this with pencil and paper. My first question: ГўВҖВңCan I assume I have a prime number generator or would you like me to write one?ГўВҖВқ The look of shock on the interviewers face was priceless, but he told me I could assume I have one. After a couple of extra questions, I started writing the function. At one point, I used an array an array to capture the prime factors and then erased it, replacing it with a hash (a ГўВҖВңdictionaryГўВҖВқ, in many other languages). The interviewer corrected me, saying I wanted an array, and I replied that with an array, I would have to count the number of each of the prime factors, but with a hash, I would merely need to increment the values, with the keys being the primes. The interviewer was again surprised. After I was done, he desk-checked my solution to verify it. I had quickly, with almost no thought, written the solution. I looked brilliant. What the interviewer didnГўВҖВҷt know what was that about a month ago, I was disappointed that I had forgotten most of my math and bought a book to teach it to myself again. About a week prior to the interview, I had written a program, complete with a prime number generator, to solve this very problem. I didnГўВҖВҷt get a job offer because they wanted to pay me a junior developer salary. This has happened to me more than once. So I still havenГўВҖВҷt relearned the math I once knew, but I found myself getting fascinated by mathematical philosophies and how the approach the idea of whether math is ГўВҖВңrealГўВҖВқ or merely a description of real. In The Beginning When we do mathematics, itГўВҖВҷs easy to forget that weГўВҖВҷre standing on millennia of philosophical debate. Most of us were taught that math is ГўВҖВңjust true,ГўВҖВқ but behind every proof lies a question: what is mathematics? Over the last 150 years, mathematicians and philosophers have offered competing answers. Some see math as eternal truth, others as symbol manipulation, others as mental construction, and still others as a pragmatic tool that survives only because it works. Understanding these foundations matters because they shape how we build proofs, interpret results, and even design new mathematics. But first, we need to understand Plato . Plato lived roughly 400 BCE and to him, math was something to be discovered, not invented. He viewed mathematics as fundamental to understanding reality and knowledge and believed mathematical objects like numbers and geometric forms existed as perfect, eternal forms in a realm beyond the physical world. These mathematical truths were absolute and unchanging and the concept of a perfect circle or the number 2 existed independently of any physical circles or pairs of objects. In short, math to Plato was a weird blend of mysticism and science and for the next 2000 years, thatГўВҖВҷs mostly where math remained. Yes, we made tremendous advances and there were philosophical differences from AristotleГўВҖВҷs realism to KantГўВҖВҷs synthetic a priori , but these were largely philosophical debates, not mathematical. That leads us to one of the great leaps of modern math philosophies, Logicism. The Great Philosophies. HereГўВҖВҷs a timeline of these philosophies to get you started. document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => { (() => { // 1) Mount point & Shadow DOM const mount = document.getElementById("mft-embed"); if (!mount) return; const shadow = mount.attachShadow({ mode: "open" }); // 2) Static content (scoped) shadow.innerHTML = `

Timeline of Modern Mathematics

Click any label to open the related Wikipedia article. Click the timeline to view a larger version.

Realist Formal Constructive Pragmatic
`; // 3) Data & helpers (scoped) const events = [ { label: "Logicism", year: 1880, desc: "Frege, Russell: reduce math to logic", stance: "Formal", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logicism" }, { label: "Formalism", year: 1900, desc: "Hilbert: symbols & consistency", stance: "Formal", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Formalism_(philosophy_of_mathematics)" }, { label: "Intuitionism", year: 1907, desc: "Brouwer: mental constructions", stance: "Constructive", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intuitionism" }, { label: "Finitism", year: 1920, desc: "Only finite objects exist", stance: "Constructive", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Finitism" }, { label: "Constructivism", year: 1920, desc: "Predicative/constructive math", stance: "Constructive", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constructivism_in_philosophy_of_mathematics" }, { label: "Set-theoretic Platonism (ZFC)", year: 1922, desc: "ZermeloГўВҖВ“Fraenkel set theory", stance: "Realist", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zermelo%E2%80%93Fraenkel_set_theory" }, { label: "Mathematical Naturalism", year: 1950, desc: "Quine, Putnam: works for science", stance: "Pragmatic", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indispensability_argument" }, { label: "Structuralism", year: 1960, desc: "Math about structures/relations", stance: "Realist", wiki: "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Structuralism_(philosophy_of_mathematics)" }, ]; const stanceColor = (s) => ({ "Realist":"var(--realist)", "Formal":"var(--formal)", "Constructive":"var(--constructive)", "Pragmatic":"var(--pragmatic)", }[s] || "black"); // 4) Renderer (scoped) function render(container, {width, height, big=false}) { container.innerHTML = ""; const pad = 28; const baselineY = Math.round(height*0.58); const yearMin = 1850, yearMax = 2000; const xOfYear = y => pad + ((y - yearMin) / (yearMax - yearMin)) * (width - pad*2); const svgNS = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"; const svg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "svg"); svg.setAttribute("width", width); svg.setAttribute("height", height); svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${width} ${height}`); svg.setAttribute("role", "img"); svg.setAttribute("aria-label", "Foundations of mathematics timeline with clickable labels."); svg.style.background = "var(--bg)"; svg.style.borderRadius = "14px"; svg.style.boxShadow = big ? "var(--shadow)" : "none"; svg.style.userSelect = "none"; // Axis const axis = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "line"); axis.setAttribute("x1", pad); axis.setAttribute("x2", width - pad); axis.setAttribute("y1", baselineY); axis.setAttribute("y2", baselineY); axis.setAttribute("stroke", "var(--axis)"); axis.setAttribute("stroke-width", 3); axis.setAttribute("stroke-dasharray", "6 6"); svg.appendChild(axis); // Ticks for(let y=1860; y<=2000; y+=20){ const x = xOfYear(y); const t = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "text"); t.setAttribute("x", x); t.setAttribute("y", baselineY + 20); t.setAttribute("text-anchor", "middle"); t.setAttribute("fill", "var(--muted)"); t.setAttribute("font-size", big ? 14 : 12); t.textContent = y; svg.appendChild(t); const tick = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "line"); tick.setAttribute("x1", x); tick.setAttribute("x2", x); tick.setAttribute("y1", baselineY-6); tick.setAttribute("y2", baselineY+6); tick.setAttribute("stroke", "var(--muted)"); tick.setAttribute("stroke-width", 1); svg.appendChild(tick); } // Layout const data = [...events].sort((a,b)=>a.year-b.year).map((d,i)=>({ ...d, side: (i%2===0) ? "top" : "bottom" })); const laneGapX = 110; const laneStep = big ? 72 : 56; const bubbleW = (d)=> Math.min(340, Math.max(130, (big? 6.9:6.5)*(d.label.length + d.desc.length*0.9))); const lanesTop = [], lanesBot = []; function placeLane(d){ const x = xOfYear(d.year); const lanes = d.side==="top" ? lanesTop : lanesBot; let lane = 0; while(true){ if(!lanes[lane]){ lanes[lane] = { right:-1e9 }; break; } if(x - lanes[lane].right > laneGapX){ break; } lane++; } lanes[lane] = { right: x + bubbleW(d) * 0.55 }; d.lane = lane; } data.forEach(placeLane); // Events data.forEach(d=>{ const x = xOfYear(d.year); const color = stanceColor(d.stance); const point = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "circle"); point.setAttribute("cx", x); point.setAttribute("cy", baselineY); point.setAttribute("r", big ? 9 : 7); point.setAttribute("fill", color); point.setAttribute("class", "mft-event-point"); svg.appendChild(point); const stem = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "line"); const dir = d.side==="top" ? -1 : 1; const stemLen = (big? 36:28) + d.lane*laneStep; stem.setAttribute("x1", x); stem.setAttribute("x2", x); stem.setAttribute("y1", baselineY); stem.setAttribute("y2", baselineY + dir*stemLen); stem.setAttribute("stroke", color); stem.setAttribute("stroke-width", 2.5); svg.appendChild(stem); const bw = bubbleW(d); const bh = big ? 64 : 56; const bx = Math.round(x - bw/2); const by = Math.round(baselineY + dir*(stemLen + 8) - (d.side==="top"? bh : 0)); const fo = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "foreignObject"); fo.setAttribute("x", Math.max(6, Math.min(bx, width-bw-6))); fo.setAttribute("y", by); fo.setAttribute("width", bw); fo.setAttribute("height", bh+6); const div = document.createElement("div"); div.setAttribute("xmlns","http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"); div.className = "mft-bubble"; div.style.borderLeft = `4px solid ${color}`; div.innerHTML = ` ${d.label}
${d.desc}
${d.stance}
`; fo.appendChild(div); svg.appendChild(fo); const yearText = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "text"); yearText.setAttribute("x", x); yearText.setAttribute("y", baselineY - (d.side==="top" ? 10 : -18)); yearText.setAttribute("text-anchor", "middle"); yearText.setAttribute("fill", "#243b53"); yearText.setAttribute("font-size", big ? 13 : 12); yearText.textContent = d.year; svg.appendChild(yearText); }); // Decorative badge (only in large view) if(big){ const bx=width*0.15, by=26, bw=width*0.34, bh=38; const rect = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "rect"); rect.setAttribute("x", bx); rect.setAttribute("y", by); rect.setAttribute("width", bw); rect.setAttribute("height", bh); rect.setAttribute("rx", 10); rect.setAttribute("fill", "#fff7ed"); rect.setAttribute("stroke", "#fdba74"); svg.appendChild(rect); const t1 = document.createElementNS(svgNS,"text"); t1.setAttribute("x", bx+bw/2); t1.setAttribute("y", by+16); t1.setAttribute("text-anchor","middle"); t1.setAttribute("font-size","13"); t1.textContent = "Ancient Platonism (~400 BCE)"; const t2 = document.createElementNS(svgNS,"text"); t2.setAttribute("x", bx+bw/2); t2.setAttribute("y", by+32); t2.setAttribute("text-anchor","middle"); t2.setAttribute("font-size","12"); t2.setAttribute("fill","#374151"); t2.textContent = "Idea: mathematics exists independently of humans"; svg.appendChild(t1); svg.appendChild(t2); } if(!big){ svg.style.cursor = "zoom-in"; svg.addEventListener("click", openLightbox, { once:false }); } container.appendChild(svg); } // 5) Wire up within shadow root const smallRoot = shadow.getElementById("mft-timeline"); const largeRoot = shadow.getElementById("mft-timeline-large"); const btnOpen = shadow.getElementById("mft-open-large"); const lb = shadow.getElementById("mft-lightbox"); const btnClose = shadow.getElementById("mft-close"); function openLightbox(){ lb.classList.add("open"); render(largeRoot, { width: Math.min(1400, window.innerWidth-120), height: Math.min(820, window.innerHeight-160), big: true }); btnClose && btnClose.focus(); } function closeLightbox(){ lb.classList.remove("open"); } btnOpen.addEventListener("click", openLightbox); btnClose.addEventListener("click", closeLightbox); lb.addEventListener("click", (e) => { if (e.target === lb) closeLightbox(); }); const onKey = (e) => { if (e.key === "Escape") closeLightbox(); }; document.addEventListener("keydown", onKey); // 6) Responsive sizing const ro = new ResizeObserver(() => { const parentWidth = mount.parentElement?.clientWidth || 600; const w = Math.min(parentWidth, 1100); render(smallRoot, { width: w, height: 360 }); }); ro.observe(mount); // Initial paint const w0 = Math.min(1100, mount.clientWidth || 1100); render(smallRoot, { width: w0, height: 360 }); // Cleanup const mo = new MutationObserver(() => { if (!document.body.contains(mount)) { ro.disconnect(); document.removeEventListener("keydown", onKey); mo.disconnect(); } }); mo.observe(document.body, { childList:true, subtree:true }); })(); }); Logicism Logicism was a school of thought pioneered by Gottlob Frege in the late 19 th century and argues that mathematics is fundamentally reducible to logic. FregeГўВҖВҷs groundbreaking work, especially in his Begriffsschrift and The Foundations of Arithmetic, tried to show that numbers and arithmetic truths are not dependent on human intuition or experience but can be derived entirely from logical principles. Although FregeГўВҖВҷs system was famously undermined by Bertrand RussellГўВҖВҷs paradox , his project reshaped the philosophy of mathematic. Both Frege and Russel had hoped to reduce math to pure logic, GГғВ¶delГўВҖВҷs incompleteness theorems showed that there are always ГўВҖВңtrueГўВҖВқ statements about natural numbers which cannot be proven. Worse, GГғВ¶delГўВҖВҷs second theorem proved no consistent set of axioms can prove its own consistency. Formalism HilbertГўВҖВҷs formalism, developed by German mathematician David Hilbert in the early twentieth century, was an ambitious attempt to establish mathematics on completely secure foundations by treating it as a purely formal system of symbol manipulation. Hilbert wanted to show the consistency of all mathematical systems by formalizing them using a specific language that included variables, quantifiers, equality, logical connectives, and undefined parameters, with the goal of proving that every theorem could be derived using nothing more than axioms and formal rules without any reference to meaning or interpretation. While logicists believed that numbers are genuine logical objects with inherent meaning and that mathematical statements express logical truths, formalism treats mathematics as purely syntactic manipulation of meaningless symbols according to explicit rules, like an elaborate game of chess. Where logicists sought to ground mathematics in logical truth and abstract reality, formalists explicitly abandon any semantic content, viewing mathematical validity purely in terms of rule-following within formal systems without any insistence on the existence of mathematical objects or their connection to logic. As you might guess, GГғВ¶delГўВҖВҷs incompleteness theorems wrecked formalism, too. Intuitionism Perhaps my favorite mathematical philosophy is intuitionism , created by L. E. J. Brouwer , intuitionism argues that math is only a construct of the mind. BrouwerГўВҖВҷs was a rebel philosophy that thumbed its nose at the big mathematical worldviews of his time. Platonists like Russell believe mathematicians is discovering eternal truths that exist independently in some abstract realm. Formalists like Hilbert see math as an elaborate game of symbol manipulation governed by rules (with no need to worry about what the symbols actually ГўВҖВңmeanГўВҖВқ). Logicists try to reduce mathematics to pure logic. Brouwer said ГўВҖВңnopeГўВҖВқ to all of it. His radical claim was that mathematical objects donГўВҖВҷt pre-exist waiting to be discovered (contra Platonism), arenГўВҖВҷt just meaningless symbols pushed around according to rules (contra formalism), and canГўВҖВҷt be reduced to logical principles (contra logicism). Brouwer argued that mathematical objects come into existence through mental construction. When you prove something or define a number, youГўВҖВҷre not finding it, playing with symbols, or deriving it from logic, youГўВҖВҷre actually creating it in your mind through intuitive mental activity. He rejected fundamental principles that the other schools took for granted, like the law of excluded middle, because for Brouwer, you canГўВҖВҷt assume something exists mathematically unless you can constructively build it step by step. It was a philosophy that made math deeply personal and creative, while simultaneously making it much more restrictive than classical mathematics allowed. Wait? What the heck is the law of the excluded middle ? In classical logic, every proposition is either true or false. Brouwer rejects that until you can actually construct a proof that shows which one it is. Think about it this way: imagine I claim ГўВҖВңthere exists a prime number with exactly 1,000 digits that has some weird property X.ГўВҖВқ In classical math, most people would say this statement is either true or false; we just donГўВҖВҷt know which yet. Brouwer would say ГўВҖВңhold up, that statement doesnГўВҖВҷt have a truth value until someone actually constructs such a number or proves none exists.ГўВҖВқ ItГўВҖВҷs the mathematical equivalent of ГўВҖВңpics or it didnГўВҖВҷt happen.ГўВҖВқ Or to put it another way, IГўВҖВҷm currently sitting in ChГғВўteauneuf-Grasse, in the south of France. I could easily say, ГўВҖВңit is raining here a year from now.ГўВҖВқ But is that true? It is false? Brouwer would argue that itГўВҖВҷs neither until we observe (construct) whether itГўВҖВҷs raining. This is a key insight of BrouwerГўВҖВҷs: math is essentially about time. Mathematical truth isnГўВҖВҷt some eternal, timeless thing floating in PlatoГўВҖВҷs heaven. Math unfolds through our mental activity over time. When we construct a proof, weГўВҖВҷre not discovering a pre-existing truth, weГўВҖВҷre creating truth through a temporal process of mental construction. This sounds reasonable enough until you realize how many classical proofs rely on saying ГўВҖВңwell, either P is true or not-P is true, so letГўВҖВҷs consider both cases...ГўВҖВқ Brouwer basically banned this move unless you could constructively demonstrate which case actually holds. The result was a much more restrictive but philosophically purer mathematics where every theorem came with a recipe for actually building or computing the objects it talked about. In fact, while most of basic math remains the same, BrouwerГўВҖВҷs insistence upon time meant that he rejected the concept of actual infinity. IГўВҖВҷve chatted with mathematicians about this and their general opinion, if theyГўВҖВҷve ever heard about Brouwer, is that the ideas are useless but since so much of math depends on the law of the excluded middle (and proof by contradition, something else intuitionism rejects), that intuitionism isnГўВҖВҷt practical. [3] But what about irrational numbers like ГҸВҖ (pi)? They have infinitely many digits after the decimal point, right? Well, weГўВҖВҷve calculated trillions of them, but even NASA only uses the first 15 digits after the decimal point . This fits well with my thoughts on the philosophy of math. ItГўВҖВҷs not so much that we arrive at the correct answer as we arrive at the useful answer. For example, $\sqrt{25}$ is equal to $5$ or $-5$. Which is correct? If youГўВҖВҷre using this to measure distances, $-5$ doesnГўВҖВҷt make any sense, even thought itГўВҖВҷs an ГўВҖВңanswer.ГўВҖВқ More Philophical Approaches. Set-theoretic Platonism (ZFC) This is todayГўВҖВҷs ГўВҖВңworking foundationГўВҖВқ for most mathematicians. Everything is built from sets. ItГўВҖВҷs powerful but incomplete. Questions like the Continuum Hypothesis canГўВҖВҷt be resolved within it. Structuralism Instead of objects, math is about relations . The number 2, for example, is just a position in the natural number structure. This view resonates with category theory and modern abstract algebra. Finitism and Constructivism More cautious views that reject infinite or circular definitions. They anticipate todayГўВҖВҷs emphasis on computability. (Note: Constructivism was invented after Intuitionism, but the latter is considered a subset of the former). Mathematical Naturalism W. V. Quine and Hilary Putnam argued that math doesnГўВҖВҷt need metaphysical justification. It survives because science canГўВҖВҷt do without it. Why It Matters You might wonder: isnГўВҖВҷt this just philosophy? Does it affect actual math? The answer is yes . These foundations influence: What counts as a proof. An intuitionist wonГўВҖВҷt accept a non-constructive proof, while a formalist doesnГўВҖВҷt care as long as the rules are followed. How we build new fields. Set theory, category theory, and constructive type theory all stem from these debates. How math connects to computers and science. Constructive philosophies align with algorithms, while empiricism connects math to physics and engineering. The result is not just an academic quarrel, but a living set of options that shape mathematics itself. Do Mathematicians Care? Not really. Most mathematicians donГўВҖВҷt spend their days debating whether theyГўВҖВҷre Platonists or Formalists. In practice, they adopt a kind of pragmatic Platonism , treating numbers, sets, and functions as if they exist ГўВҖВңout thereГўВҖВқ and using them without worrying too much about metaphysics. But the foundations arenГўВҖВҷt just idle philosophy. They matter in three key moments: When crises arise: At the turn of the 20th century, paradoxes in set theory forced Hilbert, Brouwer, and Russell to rethink the entire basis of mathematics. When new frameworks are born: Category theory, constructive type theory, and other modern tools grew out of foundational debates and now drive progress in algebra, logic, and computer science. When limits are exposed: Independence results (like the Continuum Hypothesis) raise the question of whether some problems are even meaningful, depending on your stance. So while most mathematicians can happily ignore these questions day-to-day, the edges of mathematics, where paradoxes and new ideas appear, are grown from the foundational soil beneath. Closing Thought So is mathematics discovered or invented? Despite the Platonist intuition that math exists ГўВҖВңout there,ГўВҖВқ to me the evidence points toward invention: sophisticated mental constructions we create to describe what we observe. Consider how weГўВҖВҷve repeatedly adjusted our mathematical frameworks when reality didnГўВҖВҷt cooperate. Euclidean geometry seemed eternal and absolute until we discovered that parallel lines actually do meet in curved spacetime, leading to non-Euclidean geometries that better describe our universe. Newtonian mechanics worked perfectly until quantum phenomena and relativistic effects demanded entirely new mathematical languages. Brouwer was onto something profound: mathematics unfolds through human mental activity over time. We donГўВҖВҷt find pre-existing mathematical truths; we construct tools that help us make sense of patterns in the world. When those tools fail to capture what we observe, we invent better ones. More importantly, ideas such as BrouwerГўВҖВҷs intuitionism might have real-world applications. HereГўВҖВҷs quantum physicist Nicolas Gisin using intuitionism to show that time might have a direction after all. Or if you prefer reading, hereГўВҖВҷs a short article explaining GisinГўВҖВҷs point of view . Or hereГўВҖВҷs his short and easy-to-read paper on the topic (pdf). Unfortunately, the Brouwer-Hilbert debate was won by Hilbert and time was essentially ejected from physics (read the Gisin paper).
title: Is Math Discovered or Invented?

body: Introduction  Get Your Papers in Order  The Four-Step Plan  1. Laying the Foundation  Building your RГғВ©sumГғВ©/CV  Generative AI  Google Thyself  Get Your Friends to Help  2. Applying for Jobs  Other Employment Finding Strategies  Applying  The Cover Letter  3. Handling the Phone Interview  The three dangerous questions  4. Negotiating the Salary  The Employment Contract  Conclusion     Introduction  For background, IГўВҖВҷm an American and have lived in the US, Japan, the UK, the Netherlands, France, and am currently writing this from Malta. I also spent years researching and writing a ГўВҖВңhow to move to a foreign countryГўВҖВқ blog. I considered writing a book on the topic, or touring the US and giving seminars, but IГўВҖВҷve a wife, a daughter, a career, and a habit of moving from country to country. IГўВҖВҷm busy.  That being said, the situation in the United States is getting tense and people are wondering if they can move to another country. They are asking about my now defunct Overseas Exile  blog and others have reached out to me directly. IГўВҖВҷll try to write more as I have timeГўВҖВ”though IГўВҖВҷm very busy right nowГўВҖВ”but letГўВҖВҷs get into it. This will probably be my longest blog entry to date, but itГўВҖВҷs worth it. This is the information you need to know.  First, if youГўВҖВҷre not a skilled worker, you might want to read my Young PersonГўВҖВҷs Guide to Moving Abroad . If you think you are a skilled worker, read on.  For many of you reading, youГўВҖВҷre probably frustrated. You want to live and work in a foreign country, but youГўВҖВҷre not reading this to teach English in South Korea or be a nanny in Brazil. YouГўВҖВҷre educated, highly skilled, and you think you have skills that an employer overseas might value. In short, you want to find someone who will sponsor your work permit. YouГўВҖВҷre sick of the rat race in your home country and you long for a better life in Thailand where your nice income will let you enjoy time on relaxing, pollution-free beaches.  The problem is that you donГўВҖВҷt know where to start. There are some things youГўВҖВҷve heard of, such as being a hot-shot CEO or working for a multinational and hoping theyГўВҖВҷll transfer you, but you donГўВҖВҷt think thatГўВҖВҷs going to work for you. This guide is a four-step program for getting a company abroad to offer you a job. ItГўВҖВҷs what I used and others I know have successfully used this. I counseled many people who tried to move abroad when my former blog was very popular.   Laying the Foundation  Applying for Jobs  Handling the Phone Interview  Negotiating the Salary   None of this is rocket science. None of this is quick and easy, either. ItГўВҖВҷs also not guaranteed. A variation of the described technique got me two job offers in London (and would gotten me a third if I hadnГўВҖВҷt already accepted a job in Nottingham, UK) and a standing offer in Paris which I later accepted after moving to the UK. It works, but you have to put in the effort.  But first, we need to take a little detour. YouГўВҖВҷll want to read my article Why YouГўВҖВҷll Say ГўВҖВңNoГўВҖВқ to Living Abroad , and then get your papers in order.  Get Your Papers in Order  My wife is French, IГўВҖВҷm American and we got married at the Tower Bridge in London. This involved the nightmare of trying to coordinate the paperwork of three countries to make sure our marriage was legal. If youГўВҖВҷre like me, you hate paperwork but in the international arena, it becomes far more important than many people suspect.  For example, in the United States theyГўВҖВҷve tried to limit ID theft with vital record cross-referencing. Since many identity thieves used to steal the identity of someone already dead (hey, theyГўВҖВҷre not going to complain!), it was thought to be a good idea to join death certificates with birth certificates so that a re-issued birth certificate of a deceased person would, in fact, be marked deceased. This works well if the deceased died in the state they were born, but imagine what happens when a clerk in Montana gets a death certificate from a clerk in Vermont. They donГўВҖВҷt know each other, they donГўВҖВҷt know each otherГўВҖВҷs paperwork and the pain of accidentally marking a living person as dead is just too great (I should know: my mother thought it hilarious when she received paperwork telling her that her very alive husband wasnГўВҖВҷt).  Now try to imagine the difficulty of different countries trying to verify each otherГўВҖВҷs paperwork. ItГўВҖВҷs a nightmare of different languages, forms, legal frameworks, etc. But you can have a running head start before it starts chasing you. Again, I remind you of the lesson of Count von Europe: if you had an opportunity to leave tomorrow, would you really want to say ГўВҖВңno thanks, I donГўВҖВҷt have all the paperwork.ГўВҖВқ  At a bare minimum, you need to have the following for everyone involved:   Passports  Birth certificates  Marriage certificates  Death certificates   You might need copies of university degrees or certifications if theyГўВҖВҷre relevant to your new job.  If you have a rough strategy for getting out and you know where you are going, you can plan ahead and research their requirements. You honestly donГўВҖВҷt want to be this poor bloke  who found out that he had to have his ex-wifeГўВҖВҷs birth certificate before he could claim dual Portuguese/US citizenship (read the rest of that guyГўВҖВҷs story to get an idea of how much ГўВҖВңfunГўВҖВқ international paperwork can be).  YouГўВҖВҷre also going to have to learn what an apostille  is. An apostille is akin to the stamp a notary public might issue but itГўВҖВҷs recognized by the countries which have signed on to the requisite Hague Convention. It really does make your life simpler and itГўВҖВҷs one case of international law making lives easier.  Generally speaking, you want to have these documents on hand with apostilles attached. However, this is not always appropriate. As my wife (then fiancГғВ©e, obviously) and I were trying to sort through the legal issues of three countries recognizing our marriage, we found that for some documents, France would not accept them unless they had been issued within the last six months. Some things are going to be hard no matter what you do.  Now, letГўВҖВҷs find you a job.  The Four-Step Plan  1. Laying the Foundation  YouГўВҖВҷre either a ГўВҖВңskilledГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңunskilledГўВҖВқ worker. The terms are vague, but loosely involve the amount of training/education needed for a particular skill. You may be the best pizza delivery guy in your country, but youГўВҖВҷre still going to be considered an ГўВҖВңunskilledГўВҖВқ worker. Unskilled workers are going to have to go a different route to working overseas (such as teaching English), but this post is going to concentrate on what skilled workers need for a work permit job.  Building your RГғВ©sumГғВ©/CV  The first thing you have to do is to build your rГғВ©sumГғВ©. This is your key to making things happen. Early in 2006, I dropped by a former employer in Portland to pick up some paperwork I needed for my UK work permit. One of my former colleagues asked: ГўВҖВңwhy do you get to go to Europe and we donГўВҖВҷt?ГўВҖВқ  I replied, quite honestly, ГўВҖВңI know some of you are better programmers than I am, but nobody knows who you are.ГўВҖВқ  In other words: I marketed myself. I constantly answered questions for people online and posted advice for them on appropriate forums. I did this for years and still do it on different forums. You wonГўВҖВҷt necessarily have to do that (or do that for years), but you do need to figure out some way of ГўВҖВңmarketingГўВҖВқ yourself so that you stand out. What this means is that you need to be competent in your field but you donГўВҖВҷt have to be the absolute best. HereГўВҖВҷs where a multi-prong strategy helps. In addition to getting myself known online, I also ...   Volunteered with The Perl Foundation, a non-profit organization  Spoke at conferences  Wrote articles for web sites  Coauthored a book: Perl Hacks: Tips & Tools for Programming, Debugging, and Surviving   Wrote a Beginning Perl  book (er, after I moved abroad)   These are all on my CV. These sorts of things may not apply to you, but you need to find something which will. For example, are you a social worker? How many social workers in the US do you know who have joined the National Association of Social Workers? Did you know that many magazines and Web sites are struggling to find new writers? If youГўВҖВҷre not sure who you would submit article proposals to, grab a copy of WriterГўВҖВҷs Market  .  Remember that the goal is to have a rГғВ©sumГғВ© which makes someone say ГўВҖВңhey Inga, take a look at this!ГўВҖВқ This means having something on there that other applicants donГўВҖВҷt.  Let me repeat that: have something on there that other applicants donГўВҖВҷt. In fact, have several somethings. I have an entire section entitled ГўВҖВңNotable PublicationsГўВҖВқ and I have other accomplishments in another section. My rГғВ©sumГғВ© gets noticed.  And try to make it relevant to your career. If youГўВҖВҷre a jet mechanic, volunteering at a homeless shelter is nice, but if youГўВҖВҷre trying to get a job as a volunteer coordinator, itГўВҖВҷs a lot nicer. Anything which is both positive and career-related should show up on that rГғВ©sumГғВ©, particularly if itГўВҖВҷs something other applicants wonГўВҖВҷt have (at the same time, a 37 page rГғВ©sumГғВ© is going straight into the trash after everyone laughs at it).  Generative AI  If generative AI is relevant in your field, learn it . More and more employers are seeking people with AI skills. The Daily AI Brief  is business focused and there are tons of free, online courses for learning prompt engineering. The Neuron Daily has a decent one  , though itГўВҖВҷs out of date (theyГўВҖВҷre always out of date), but it gives you place to start.  Google Thyself  While youГўВҖВҷre at it, hit the major search engines and search for your name. If you search for my name, most of those links are me. If you search for my name and ГўВҖВңPerlГўВҖВқ, the programming language I specialize in, almost all of those links are me. That was me deliberately marketing myself. People will search for your name. What will they find? If they find dishing out snotty, off-color remarks and being rude to people, or find that photo of you vomiting at a frat party, youГўВҖВҷre not going to get the job. I know some people in my field who are very competent and well-known, but because theyГўВҖВҷre rude online, they lose employment opportunities. Even if youГўВҖВҷre not going to spend a lot of time online, take care of that online image. Every time you post something think ГўВҖВңwould I want an employer to see this?ГўВҖВқ  Get Your Friends to Help  While youГўВҖВҷre at it, have your friends Google you ГўВҖВ” and read your rГғВ©sumГғВ©/CV ГўВҖВ” to look for things that youГўВҖВҷll miss. You canГўВҖВҷt afford to screw up here. A single misspelled word may be quietly killing your dreams.  Also, throw a ГўВҖВңgo awayГўВҖВқ party with them. YouГўВҖВҷll just sit around and get stupidly drunk (or whatever it is that you do) and brainstorm ideas for things you can get on your CV (failing that, ask Reddit!) Get those creative juices flowing so you can figure out how to make this work.  Is this hard work? Yup. I never promised it would be easy, but itГўВҖВҷs not going to happen without effort on your part.  2. Applying for Jobs  OK, youГўВҖВҷve started laying the foundations for getting a job in another country. Now what? Find the damned country.  The first thing you need to do is develop a plan. ItГўВҖВҷs easier if you target just one country as you can become more familiar with it. You need to be familiar with:   Shortage occupations  Work permit process  Local news  Employer advertisements   Many countries have shortage occupation lists  , though the name of the list often varies. These are jobs which the country doesnГўВҖВҷt have enough workers for and foreign workers (thatГўВҖВҷs you!) are more likely to get hired for them. YouГўВҖВҷd be surprised at some of the jobs on them. For example, the UK shortage occupation list used to have social workers in child and family services, but who would have thought that ГўВҖВңsocial workerГўВҖВқ is a high demand job?  Review these lists for the countries youГўВҖВҷre interested in and ask yourself one question: ГўВҖВңcould I get hired in this job in my home country?ГўВҖВқ If the answer is ГўВҖВңyesГўВҖВқ, youГўВҖВҷve got a winner. These are high-demand jobs in those countries and theyГўВҖВҷre much easier to get work permits for. Because theyГўВҖВҷre shortage occupation lists, employers are often more willing to be flexible.  Next, learn everything you can about the work permit process for that country. Want to live in the UK? Start by reading everything on their official Visas and Immigration site  . I said ГўВҖВңstartГўВҖВқ. YouГўВҖВҷre not going to finish there. You have to keep reading and reading and reading. You have to know their work permit process backwards and forwards. You might think ГўВҖВңbut the employer is the one who has to know this, right?ГўВҖВқ Sure, but trust me. All will be clear soon.  Next, you will start reading their local news religiously and getting to know your target area. This includes scanning job adverts (but not applying yet). You see, you need to know your target area very well. You need to know the markets. You need to have a comfortable level of knowledge to discuss the place. This will help for a couple of reasons. First, youГўВҖВҷll start to understand where the jobs are. Second, when you get a phone interview, you might find ГўВҖВңlocalГўВҖВқ discussions occurring and you want to sound knowledgeable. Sounding like you have no idea where the hell youГўВҖВҷre going is not going to impress anyone. At this point, anything which might scare a potential employer off would be bad.  Let me rephrase this to make it absolutely clear whatГўВҖВҷs going on: youГўВҖВҷre trying to get a job in a foreign country. You have to prep for these job interviews like you never have before in your life. Every question should be an opportunity for you to hit a home run. You know the country. You know the industry. You know the company, their competition, and anything else which may be relevant. You are the most prepared person on the damned planet for that job. Or at least, thatГўВҖВҷs what the employer needs to think.  Mind you, if you get an offer, youГўВҖВҷll find that you probably didnГўВҖВҷt need all of the knowledge you had, but you wonГўВҖВҷt know which bits you wonГўВҖВҷt need. DonГўВҖВҷt take chances here: learn everything.  Other Employment Finding Strategies  ItГўВҖВҷs worth noting that some countries donГўВҖВҷt have shortage lists but you might want to move there anyway. ThatГўВҖВҷs OK. Just keep reading about their residency and work permit requirements, along with their news. After a while, a picture of the countryГўВҖВҷs economy will start forming and youГўВҖВҷll get ideas about where useful skills might be needed. Or you might get lucky and discover a country like Uruguay where all you need is a small, steady income and youГўВҖВҷre good to go. Remember: the key to this is to be flexible and think creatively.  You should also start hanging out on ГўВҖВңexpatГўВҖВқ message boards for your target country. Just type ГўВҖВңCOUNTRYNAME expatГўВҖВқ into your favorite search engine and you should find plenty of them. DonГўВҖВҷt sign up at first, just start reading through them. This will give you a sense of what other expats are doing, the problems they face, and most importantly (for now) the type of work they found. Once you have a feel for what the appropriate etiquette is for the boards you are reading, sign up and join in the discussion if itГўВҖВҷs appropriate. If the boards welcome people looking to move there, you might find expats willing to offer ГўВҖВңinsideГўВҖВқ advice. Just like working on your rГғВ©sumГғВ©, youГўВҖВҷre applying a multi-prong strategy here.  Finally, just check this list of international job web sites. ItГўВҖВҷs not complete, but itГўВҖВҷs a good sample of jobs for you.  Applying  HereГўВҖВҷs the scary part. YouГўВҖВҷve practically packed your bags, you have your papers in order, you have a CV which stands out (and is formatted as your target country prefers), youГўВҖВҷve picked your target country and you know their market like the back of your hand. You know youГўВҖВҷre a great candidate. ItГўВҖВҷs time to apply for jobs and this is where things break down for people. They donГўВҖВҷt know what to do. What I would tell them is ГўВҖВңthink of Charlie.ГўВҖВқ Charlie is a guy I knew in Alaska who had a habit of getting jobs he wasnГўВҖВҷt qualified for. At one point, he was even a bank manager though his main skill was carpentry. How did he get these jobs?  He applied for them.  ItГўВҖВҷs honestly that simple. Admittedly, Charlie had the gift of gab and could talk his way into these jobs, but no employer is ever going to say yes unless you ask. DonГўВҖВҷt be afraid. Just pick up the job listings for your target country ГўВҖВ” if you donГўВҖВҷt know where to find them by now, youГўВҖВҷre probably applying too soon ГўВҖВ” and start sending your CV to the jobs you want.  However, thereГўВҖВҷs an even better trick if you can pull it off. Research their employees on LinkedIn or other sites (employees who have related skills, hopefully) and see if you can connect with them. Having Giorgi forward your resume from inside the company is far better than just applying directly! That being said, I applied directly for my first job in the UK, but IГўВҖВҷm pretty well-known in my field thanks to the tips I used earlier.  The Cover Letter  In todayГўВҖВҷs day and age, the cover letter seems a lost art. YouГўВҖВҷre going to revive it. For every job you apply for, you will include a cover letter. You will contact the company first and find out to whom you will be addressing this letter. In the actual letter, you will mention their company by name, tell them why you want the position, why they want to hire you, and drop details of what you know about them, their competitors, and their market. You have to be very careful with this letter. If itГўВҖВҷs too long, people wonГўВҖВҷt read it, but you have to convey enough that they pay more attention to your rГғВ©sumГғВ© than they usually would.  If youГўВҖВҷre not sure about how to write one, try having Claude write a first draft  . Explain what youГўВҖВҷre trying to do, give Claude all of the relevant information, and youГўВҖВҷll soon have a good starting point.  What you donГўВҖВҷt do in your cover letter is tell them that you need sponsorship for a work permit. One of the things which was drilled into me repeatedly in car sales (yeah, I used to sell cars, too) is that you never bring up anything negative unless someone asks. TheyГўВҖВҷre already going to see that you live in another country. Your goal is to convince them that they have to have you.  Depending on your field and your qualifications, you may have to send out quite a few rГғВ©sumГғВ©s. You may get several callbacks right away or you may have to wait months. Even if you get several callbacks, these could easily be the strangest phone interviews youГўВҖВҷve ever had. In 2001, I got a job in Amsterdam, even after one interviewer, knowing IГўВҖВҷm originally from Texas, asked if I liked to carry weapons. TheyГўВҖВҷll have stereotypes, too. You have to be prepared for these interviews and thatГўВҖВҷs what weГўВҖВҷll cover in the next part.  Eventually youГўВҖВҷll get a phone interview and theyГўВҖВҷll ask if you can legally work in their country and thatГўВҖВҷs actually fantastic. It means that your rГғВ©sumГғВ© was noticed. It also means you need know part three, how to handle the interview.  3. Handling the Phone Interview  Note: some employers will fly you out to meet them. This is the exception, not the rule. Also, the following applies to video calls, too.  So, you have a great rГғВ©sumГғВ©, youГўВҖВҷve done your research, youГўВҖВҷve applied for jobs in your target country and you get an email in your inbox asking if you can be ready for an interview at 9 AM on Monday morning.  Of course you can. YouГўВҖВҷve worked your tail off for this moment and youГўВҖВҷre not going to blow it.  However, they might. I was once woken up by my phone ringing at 3AM because the interviewer got their time zone wrong ГўВҖВ” and that was after I sent back a confirmation email to verify the time in both my time zone and theirs. Of course I still did the interview, but are you going to be prepared enough to handle an interview if youГўВҖВҷre woken up like that? Yes, you are.  YouГўВҖВҷre going to have a friend or two call you and give you an interview over the phone or by video. They need to grill you hard and make sure they would really want to hire you. They need to hit you with any and all objections they can think of and you have to smoothly address every one of them. You will not fail this interview.  DonГўВҖВҷt stress the interview too much, though. If theyГўВҖВҷve called an international candidate, theyГўВҖВҷre already intrigued. If youГўВҖВҷre an Italian with a hobbit fetish and you desperately want to live in New Zealand, just remember that the New Zealander interviewing you may find you ГўВҖВ” and your accent ГўВҖВ” as exotic as you think of them. And this goes for Americans, too. Even though the rest of the world is inundated with US movies and TV shows, IГўВҖВҷve still had ladies in Europe tell me how much they loved my boring Midwest American accent (note to my wife: they were ugly ladies. And old. Really old.)  YouГўВҖВҷre talking to a human being on the other end of the line and if theyГўВҖВҷve taken the trouble to call someone from another country, this is not a run of the mill call. TheyГўВҖВҷre going to be as curious about you as you are about them. IГўВҖВҷve had international interviews quickly devolve into laughter and discussion of local food and politics (which can be quite dangerous).  The three dangerous questions  No matter how well the interview goes, there are three questions youГўВҖВҷre almost guaranteed to be asked and you had better nail them.   Why do you want to move to our country?  Can you legally work here?  How soon can you start?   This is where all of your research is going to pay off, but for the ГўВҖВңwhy do you want to move hereГўВҖВқ question, youГўВҖВҷre going to have to come up with your own answer.  In 2006, I was interviewing for a role in the UK and was asked why I wanted to move there, I mentioned that I had family in the UK and Germany and I wanted to be closer to them. I also mentioned that I had previously visited the UK and I loved the country. All of this was true and it made me sound like I understood what was going on and not just fantasizing.  You probably donГўВҖВҷt have relatives in the target country, so thatГўВҖВҷs out. Hopefully youГўВҖВҷve at least visited. If you havenГўВҖВҷt, you might be in for a surprise. Some people who think theyГўВҖВҷd love London discover that itГўВҖВҷs too polluted and busy for them (IГўВҖВҷve several good friends who were disappointed by the city). Not having visited hurts your chances, but if you havenГўВҖВҷt, thereГўВҖВҷs not much you can do.  At the very least, study the countryГўВҖВҷs history and current affairs like mad and explain what parts of it youГўВҖВҷre fascinated by and how much youГўВҖВҷve wanted to live there and experience it first hand.  Remember, these are people on the other end of the line; theyГўВҖВҷll probably understand that answer. Most people have a hidden adventure streak in them and thereГўВҖВҷs a good chance that youГўВҖВҷll be admired for it. However, donГўВҖВҷt just say ГўВҖВңfor the adventure.ГўВҖВқ Also, donГўВҖВҷt say anything too negative about your home country. You donГўВҖВҷt know the person on the other end of the line and if theyГўВҖВҷre turned off by politics or they happen to admire the politics of your country, you may have sunk your boat. Stay positive!  The really problematic question is ГўВҖВңcan you legally work here?ГўВҖВқ This one will also make or break you. If possible, I like to kick start an interview by asking questions of the interviewer. I like to turn the interview into a dialogue, a friendly chat, and forestall this question as long as possible. If itГўВҖВҷs addressed too early, you may not get a chance to sell yourself. When itГўВҖВҷs asked, though, the answer is simple:  ГўВҖВңI need work permit before I start, but fortunately, the process is pretty straightforward.ГўВҖВқ  This is why youГўВҖВҷve spent so much time learning exactly how the countryГўВҖВҷs work permit system is structured. Explain how they can hire you. When the company in Nottingham wanted to hire me, they were concerned that it would be difficult. I assured them that it wasnГўВҖВҷt and briefly outlined the process. Then I asked for contact information in the company to whom I should explain the process in more detail. I told them where to download forms, what fees they would be paying and what papers they would need to provide. In short, I told my employer how to hire me.   [1]  At this point if theyГўВҖВҷre wavering, you need to close the deal. If you have a sales background, you should isolate their objections and deal with them one by one, but there are some issues here. First, you may not be trained to do this. Second, you probably donГўВҖВҷt know their culture and you may accidentally give offense. Third, they may simply be too polite to tell you the problem or too embarrassed to say that they donГўВҖВҷt think they can afford you.  Assuming you think that they want to hire you, you can simply deal with the most common objections outright. First, I tell them that my contract should have a provision that I will repay all or some of the relocation costs if I leave the company before a certain date. Another thing I did to guarantee my first UK move was to tell them that I only had to bring over a few suitcases: I didnГўВҖВҷt have a household full of goods to move and that reduced relocation costs considerably. They paid for a short-term flat for me, but I paid for my own plane ticket.  Finally, ГўВҖВңhow soon can you startГўВҖВқ is the easy one. YouГўВҖВҷre already sick to death of Count von Europe, but youГўВҖВҷve heeded the lesson and you have nothing holding you back. Your answer is simply ГўВҖВңI have to give X days notice to my employer. I can start as soon as my work permit is approved.ГўВҖВқ The reality is that the work permit will usually take a few months (this varies widely), so youГўВҖВҷre waiting on them rather than the other way around.  4. Negotiating the Salary  I was living in London and was back in the US for a friendГўВҖВҷs wedding and eight of us were at a Mexican restaurant for dinner. When it came time to pay the bill, eight wallets came out, with an assortment of cards and cash. I thought about it for a moment and realized that with the (then) exchange rate at $2 to the British pound and with me living in what was then the second most expensive city on the planet (or first, depending on the source), this dinner for eight was about the price of dinner for two back home. I picked up the tab and saved everyone a lot of hassle.  MoneyГўВҖВҷs always a difficult and confusing topic and if youГўВҖВҷre in the wonderful position to negotiate the salary for a job in another country, youГўВҖВҷll want to keep a number of things in mind.  Exchange rates are for exchanging money.  When I first moved overseas, I was a bit obsessed with staying on top of the exchange rate. ГўВҖВңHow much is this carton of eggs in dollars?ГўВҖВқ  It doesnГўВҖВҷt matter. What matters is whether or not something is cheap or expensive relative to the local currency, so donГўВҖВҷt stress about it unless ...  ... youГўВҖВҷre planning on returning to your home country a lot. That 30,000 baht a month salary is fantastic in Thailand but itГўВҖВҷs only about $1,000 US. If youГўВҖВҷre a Thai going to work in the US, great! If youГўВҖВҷre an American going to work in Thailand, youГўВҖВҷll want to be aware of this.  ThatГўВҖВҷs nice. Now tell me the salary to ask for.  If you live in a foreign country, youГўВҖВҷll discover that the economy is as foreign as the country. For example, when I lived in the Netherlands books were typically around ГўВӮВ¬16 to ГўВӮВ¬18 (about $21US to $24US, at that time). Mind you, weГўВҖВҷre talking about the cheap mass market paperback that will sold for $7 to $8 in the US. Living in France, many excellent wines are dirt cheap and are often far better than the beer.   [2] Petrol (gas) in Europe is far more expensive than in the US and cigarette prices vary considerably from country to country. A ГўВӮВ¬12 pack of cigarettes in France is ГўВӮВ¬5.50 in Malta.  What this means is that when youГўВҖВҷre naming a salary, youГўВҖВҷre naming a salary relative to what the job should earn, not relative to what you think youГўВҖВҷll need for your cost of living. Try as you might, you will get it wrong if you try and estimate the cost of living for yourself. Try Numbeo  if you want to understand the cost of living better. You can also check out ifitweremyhome.com  for more interesting details about your target country.  What I recommend is naming a range. This sounds strange, but it works like this. In the early 2000s, in London, many computer programmers earned between ГӮВЈ35,000 and ГӮВЈ55,000 a year. The lower end of your range should be close to the higher end of typical salaries. YouГўВҖВҷre an expert and you donГўВҖВҷt want to sell yourself short. YouГўВҖВҷll have to judge this carefully, though.  So you say, ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷd like to earn somewhere between X and Y a year, but it really depends on holiday time, working conditions, or other benefits available. What salary did you have in mind?ГўВҖВқ  At this point, youГўВҖВҷve given a range, made it clear you know what reasonable salaries are, made it clear youГўВҖВҷre flexible, and thrown it back in their lap. And youГўВҖВҷve done it very professionally. And you know what? If theyГўВҖВҷre offering eight weeks off a year, you might be quite happy to take a lower salary.  The Employment Contract  If you get this far, the actual employment contract will need some form of relocation assistance. To guarantee my job in Nottingham, while I did receive a very nice salary, I also offered to pay for my own transportation to the UK. Generally, the employer will pay for your flight and the cost (if any) of moving your goods. If youГўВҖВҷve offered to pay to secure the job, so be it (donГўВҖВҷt offer to pay unless you absolutely have to). Paying your own way means you donГўВҖВҷt have to repay if you decide to leave the position early (homesickness has ended the dreams of many an expat).  In addition to the normal terms of an employment contract, youГўВҖВҷll have to ensure that theyГўВҖВҷve guaranteed you some initial accommodation. Most larger cities offer some sort of ГўВҖВңshort-stayГўВҖВқ apartment housing for people moving to a city. They typically include a kitchen, so itГўВҖВҷs a bit more than a hotel. If youГўВҖВҷre working in a low-paying industry (e.g., social worker) and they offer to let you temporarily take a room in a coworkerГўВҖВҷs flat, consider it.  Also, if youГўВҖВҷll have trouble with rental deposits, ask the company if they can provide a payroll loan to cover it? Make sure you ask before you fly over. Being homeless is not fun (I speak from experience).  Finally, you might have to have a provision in there that if you leave before a certain amount of time, youГўВҖВҷll repay some or all of the relocation expenses. This is a normal condition, but make sure itГўВҖВҷs not for too long of a time. I probably wouldnГўВҖВҷt go over a year, but it depends on the expenses.  So thatГўВҖВҷs about it. IГўВҖВҷve covered preparing your rГғВ©sumГғВ©, researching and applying for jobs, interviewing and salary negotiations. This should give you a solid plan for attacking the foreign job market. Be aware, though, that there are plenty of other avenues to working overseas and this one is not guaranteed. ItГўВҖВҷs simply a variant of the strategy that I took and I know itГўВҖВҷs worked for others too.  Conclusion  So thatГўВҖВҷs it. A four-step plan to move abroad. I never claimed it would be easy, but you might be surprised! When I first started applying for jobs abroad, I immediately had several companies interested (including MapQuest, but they kept insisting I fly from the US to the UK on my dime for an interview, so I ignored ГўВҖВҳem).  The only problem with the above plan is that it takes a while and for many, thatГўВҖВҷs a reason not to start. DonГўВҖВҷt let it be yours.  There are also tons of other routes abroad, many of which are obscure, such as the Italian Citizenship by Descent  program. You can claim Italian citizenship if the following criteria are met:   You have male Italian ancestors living on/after March 17, 1861 could pass citizenship rights to descendants, unless they naturalized elsewhere before their childГўВҖВҷs birth  Children born after January 1, 1948 to Italian mothers qualify  No generational limit exists, provided each ancestor maintained Italian citizenship eligibility before their childГўВҖВҷs birth   There are exceptions and Italian bureaucracy gives French bureaucracy a run for its money, but it can be done. Then you buy an Italian home for $1 and live happily ever after  (hint: itГўВҖВҷs still hard work).  You can do this. If you want to live abroad, you have to put in the effort. Stop dreaming and start doing.  If youГўВҖВҷd like me to write more about how to move abroad, leave a comment below.
title: Getting a Job In Another Country

body: Introduction  IГўВҖВҷm an American whoГўВҖВҷs lived in the US, the UK, Japan, the Netherlands, and now France. People kept emailing me over the years to ask me how to move to a foreign country. As it turns out, the answer is complex and I got tired of cutting-n-pasting the same email over and over again. So I started a blog entitled ГўВҖВңOverseas Exile.ГўВҖВқ I posted several times a week for many years, but I let the domain lapse and now someone else has snapped it up. You can still read my old posts  , but I felt, given the current climate in the US, that itГўВҖВҷs worth sharing some of this here.  This wasnГўВҖВҷt my first post, but it was one of the most important, despite the silly premise.  Count von Europe  So your eccentric great-aunt Gertrude has invited you over for dinner and when you arrive, you find a mysterious stranger with her. She introduces him as ГўВҖВңCount von EuropeГўВҖВқ. After a long and pleasant conversation over food and drinks, the Count says ГўВҖВңyour aunt invited you over because I need someone to watch my Bavarian castle while IГўВҖВҷm away for a year. IГўВҖВҷll also pay you ГўВӮВ¬50,000 a year and let you borrow the Bentley when you want to travel around Europe on your days off. When can you fly to Germany?ГўВҖВқ  Sadly, for many people IГўВҖВҷve spoken with, their ГўВҖВңdreamГўВҖВқ of living abroad is little more than ГўВҖВңCount von Europe.ГўВҖВқ I see people on message boards saying things like ГўВҖВңI want to move to Italy. Anybody got a job/marriage/house for me?ГўВҖВқ Honestly, itГўВҖВҷs not going to be that easy. However, you can do it if you plan things right and understand whatГўВҖВҷs involved. When I talk to people about this, I sometimes use ГўВҖВңCount von EuropeГўВҖВқ as a thought experiment, but the ending is a little different. He makes the same offer, but with a catch: you have to leave tomorrow.  First, weГўВҖВҷll have a slight digression. Many years ago I used to sell cars (donГўВҖВҷt hate me for that. There are plenty of other things you can hate me for). One thing management drilled into our heads over and over again was that 80% of people only agree to drive home in a car after being asked to buy five times.ThatГўВҖВҷs because people have objections. ГўВҖВңThe price is too high.ГўВҖВқ ГўВҖВңI wanted red, not blue.ГўВҖВқ ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷm just looking.ГўВҖВқ My job as a salesperson was to understand and overcome all objections the customer had. If I could do that, theyГўВҖВҷd agree to buy the car, but itГўВҖВҷs harder than it sounds.  Objections  If you want to take up Count von EuropeГўВҖВҷs offer, overcome your own objections. If you can do that, youГўВҖВҷre one step closer to moving to another country, so letГўВҖВҷs look at those objections.  I donГўВҖВҷt have a passport.  This one boggles my mind because IГўВҖВҷve been surprised at how many people say they want to live in another country but donГўВҖВҷt have a passport. If youГўВҖВҷre from the US, go here to apply for a passport  . Otherwise, itГўВҖВҷs fairly easy to find out where to apply.  IГўВҖВҷm locked into a long-term lease.  Then find a way to get out of that lease. When I was offered a job in another country in 2001 I had three months left on my lease. I went to the landlord and explained the problem. She was actually very gracious about it and let me end the lease early without penalty. It never hurts to ask!  Failing that, convert your lease to a monthly lease when it ends. Or have enough cash on hand to buy out the lease. Or when it ends, become someoneГўВҖВҷs roommate.  But I own the property I live in!  Then sell it. Or rent it out and rent a room somewhere. Or find a property management company who will rent it out for you.  I have a wife/husband/partner/children, etc.  ThatГўВҖВҷs a tough one. Right now you have to decide what you want to do. If you have obligations to others who donГўВҖВҷt want to leave (IГўВҖВҷve been there and itГўВҖВҷs tough), then youГўВҖВҷre stuck. I canГўВҖВҷt/wonГўВҖВҷt offer advice here other than to suggest taking them on a vacation to the target country. ItГўВҖВҷs a lot easier to appreciate something if you know something about it.  I have a cat/dog/iguana.  You can still move overseas, but you have to make sure you conform to your target countryГўВҖВҷs regulations for shipping your pet there. The ГўВҖВңTransitions AbroadГўВҖВқ Web site has a good article to help you understand the basics of moving a pet overseas  .  Other Objections  If you get a chance to leave tomorrow, whatГўВҖВҷs stopping you? Figure out what that is and if itГўВҖВҷs more important than living abroad. And fix it.  Conclusion  Think about these objections and any others you may have. This is the starting point for being able to move overseas. If you find that you could say ГўВҖВңyesГўВҖВқ to Count von Europe, youГўВҖВҷve gotten enough of your life in order that you can make this happen. This is key to realizing the dream of moving abroad. I donГўВҖВҷt expect that youГўВҖВҷll actually be able to leave at the drop of a hat, but if you can, itГўВҖВҷs much easier. Think how much cheaper and faster it is to move to a foreign country with a backpack as compared to an entire household of goods.
title: Why You Will Say "No" to Living Abroad

body: My First Weddings  Why I Stopped Officiating Weddings  Why I Started Officiating Weddings Again  The BBC     My First Weddings  Note: Much of what follows is from memory, but the ГўВҖВҳseventeen weddings' described near the end are from local copies of old blog posts I had written on the topic, many years ago. Hence, the detail.  I have, to date, been an official participant in 21 weddings in the US since I turned 18.   [1] Twice I was the groom. The other 19 times, I was the minister. The story gets weirder from there.  My first wedding was three decades ago. It lasted five years and she and I grew apart. The divorce was difficult, but then, they often are. I hope sheГўВҖВҷs doing well.  My twenty-first, and last, wedding was in Tower Bridge, London, on June 20th, 2010, to my lovely wife LeГғВҜla. WeГўВҖВҷre still happily married, with a beautiful daughter, Lilly-Rose.     Our wedding. Source     Being a husband and father has been exhilarating, wonderful, and sometimes exhausting, but I wouldnГўВҖВҷt trade my wife and daughter for anything.  Getting back to the wedding, the date, June 20th, was selected because itГўВҖВҷs my birthday. I have no excuses if I forget. She knows me too well and I canГўВҖВҷt fault her for that.  Curiously, this is my second wedding on my birthday. The first ГўВҖВңbirthdayГўВҖВқ wedding was also the first wedding I officiated at. My friends, weГўВҖВҷll call them Alice and Bob, called me, frantic because they lost the judge who was going to perform their wedding at a lake. Apparently he was retired, senile, and had no recollection that he was to perform their wedding.  I, however, was neither old nor senile (IГўВҖВҷll not swear to either today), but I am an ordained minister. Despite being an atheist, I was ordained by The Universal Church  in September of 1990. Back then, you had to do it the hard way by sending them a postcard with your details. Today, you can do this online.  Because I was an ordained minister, Alice and Bob explained the problem and asked if I could officiate at their wedding. I told them I would call them back after I found out. I called my church and they agreed to FedexГўВ„Вў the paperwork to me, free of charge. I called my friends and told them IГўВҖВҷd be honored to preside at their wedding. I neglected to tell them it was my birthday; that would have been bad form.  I filed my paperwork with Multnomah county, in the US state of Oregon and was quickly approved, though not without some odd looks. I was wearing a pair of black earrings and a silver/grey shirt that buttoned up the side, just the sort of thing IГўВҖВҷd wear for a Goth Night of clubbing. But Portland being Portland, odd looks were all that came of that.     The first wedding I officiated at.    The wedding at the lake went off without a hitch and I got a good laugh when I pointed out that the witnesses were named ГўВҖВңTom and Jerry.ГўВҖВқ I also discovered, as a minister officiating at a wedding, itГўВҖВҷs easier to pick up women then you would think. The date went poorly and my friends later told me that had they known who I met, they would have warned me not to. (Amongst other things, on our second date, not only did I find out that she was hopped up on little pink pills her former boyfriend had brought her from Mexico, she asked me to convert to Judaism because she had recently converted and couldnГўВҖВҷt date outside of her faith. She was also an atheist. I declined a third date).  That couple also went their separate ways, so my first two weddings, one as groom and one as minister, ended in divorce.  Why I Stopped Officiating Weddings  The third wedding made me swear off officiating at weddings ever again. It was a favor for a friend of a friend. A couple couldnГўВҖВҷt afford much, so they wanted a wedding in their home. I agreed, and spoke with the couple on the phone. I asked them about the theme (ГўВҖВңnon-religiousГўВҖВқ), who theyГўВҖВҷd like to include (ГўВҖВңuh, weГўВҖВҷre not sureГўВҖВқ), if theyГўВҖВҷd be exchanging rings (ГўВҖВңwe donГўВҖВҷt knowГўВҖВқ), and when they could send me a copy of their vows (ГўВҖВңcan you write them, please?ГўВҖВң). Not one answer was particularly bad, but all of them together, coupled with very strange vibes from the phone call told me that something was, well, off.  I wrote their vows and called them back to arrange a rehearsal. They were busy, they said. Every time I called to try to arrange a rehearsal, they had an excuse. I simply could not get a rehearsal with them. So I finally said IГўВҖВҷd show up to the wedding early, meet with them, and have a quick run through of the vows. In turn, I shortened the vows, knowing this was going poorly. ItГўВҖВҷs a very good thing that I did.  The day of the wedding, my friend (er, weГўВҖВҷll call him Charlie), who asked me if I would help the couple out, drove me to their place in the middle of nowhere. It was early October and they had decided on a Halloween-themed wedding. As we pulled up the house, little white trash bags hung from the trees. I later realized they were supposed to be ghosts, but they looked for all the world like Klan hoods.  As I walked inside, I immediately assumed a ГўВҖВңministerГўВҖВқ persona, wanting to put the people at ease. Charlie introduced me to sexy vampires, unshaved cowboys, and one under-age Pocahantas   [2] who wore an ill-fitting costume and no bra. She was flashing everyone but no seemed bothered. I bit my tongue as I smiled. The phrase ГўВҖВңfeeling like a nun in a brothelГўВҖВқ never felt more appropriate.  I was ushered into the back to meet the bride and while she stood their, half-naked, she read through the vows quickly and handed them back to me, assuring me they were fine. I then met the groom in a back bedroom and he told me he was busy and had no time to read the vows. I was brushed off quickly. It felt weird. No matter. I had shortened the vows because they wouldnГўВҖВҷt meet for a rehearsal, but this was still very unsettling.  Finally the ceremony started, the happy couple faced me, their assembled family and friends faced me, I handed each of them a short copy of the vows they would read, and I started.  ГўВҖВңDearly beloved, we are gathered here today to celebrate the marriage of ...ГўВҖВқ  And that, dear reader, is when I realized that in the two months of failing to arrange a rehearsal, I had no fucking idea what their names were.  ГўВҖВң... of the lovely bride and her handsome groom.ГўВҖВқ  The audience smiled. I had gotten away with it.  I then turned to the bride and asked her to read her section of the vows. I was proud of the non-religious, life-affirming, gender-equal vows I had written. None of this, ГўВҖВңhonor and obeyГўВҖВқ nonsense. She read the vows and the audience smiled again.  I then turned to the groom and asked him to read his section of the vows. He stumbled and I discreetly pointed to the section clearly labeled ГўВҖВңgroomГўВҖВқ so he could recover.  That was when I discovered that the groom was functionally illiterate. He couldnГўВҖВҷt read the vows. He slowly, awkwardly, sounded out the words and I held a supporting smile as I died inside. He was probably dying, too. As was his fiancГғВ©e. This was a low-point in my life.  After the groom finished, I quickly got through the ГўВҖВңdo you take your bride ...ГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңdo you take your groom ...ГўВҖВңГўВҖВ”still omitting the names I didnГўВҖВҷt knowГўВҖВ”and finished with, ГўВҖВңyou may now kiss your bride.ГўВҖВқ  After the kiss finished and the applause died down, I had them turn to face the audience and said, ГўВҖВңI now introduce you to the happily married couple!ГўВҖВқ  More applause, followed by dead silence due to our lack of rehearsal. I had no idea what to do next. The audience stared at us. I stared back. I silently cursed Charlie (sorry, Charlie). Then I piped up with, ГўВҖВңhow about a question and answer session? IГўВҖВҷm sure weГўВҖВҷd all like to what their life plans are.ГўВҖВқ  ThatГўВҖВҷs me, the professional speaker. Always prepared to improvise.  After another awkward pause, the audience got into the swing of things and started peppering the bride and groom with questions as I embarrassedly slunk away to the kitchen, desperately in need of a drink. ThatГўВҖВҷs when I discovered it was a ГўВҖВңbring your own beerГўВҖВқ wedding and no one had bothered to tell me. I was filling a glass with water when some kind soul hurried up and pressed a can of room temperature Olympia beer into my hand. Olympia beer must always be served ice cold because you desperately want dead taste buds when you drink it, but I was grateful.  Later, Charlie and I stood on the back porch, smoking, drinking, and rather uncharitably speculating about how long the marriage was going to last, when the back door slammed open. An angel with a cigarette dangling from her lips burst out, cussing up a storm.
